Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Paging alert: perf-canary regression storm detected (dedupe disabled due to unique labels)

- Signal: `perf_canary_p95_latency_ms` + `perf_canary_tokens_per_sec`
- Affected: 27 alert instances across models/regions/SKU in last 5m
- Example: `model=llama-3-70b-instruct` `region=us-east` `sku=h100-80g` p95 +18% vs baseline (n=62)
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/Runbook%3A+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
- Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-region=us-east

Thread created for incident coordination. Please acknowledge.

Rafael Mendes: ack. seeing my pager light up like a christmas tree. is this real or canary gone wild?

Sean Gallagher: ack. looking at incidents channel, multiple pages within 2 mins. treating as alerting failure until proven otherwise.

Noah Patel: ack. I’m on. pulling canary logs + baseline ...

ug (30
-day windows). Prioritized groups by SLA hits and customer escalations. Short summary + suggested owners below. Full notebook: https://redwood-internal/notes/recurrence-synthesis-2026-05-aug

Jordan Blake: sure 

```
cluster_key | incidents | sla_violations | cust_escalations
KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH | 21 | 8 | 3
FALLBACK_ROLLOVER_OSC | 15 | 6 | 2
GRPC_CONN_RESET_OLD_IMAGE | 12 | 4 | 1
AUTOSCALER_SUDDEN_SCALE_DOWN | 9 | 5 | 1
EGRESS_THROTTLE_THIRD_PARTY | 6 | 3 | 2
OTHER | 10 | 2 | 0
```

Query: `-- clustering by signature + time-window + customer-impact score` (see notebook). I also computed a recurrence half-life metric per cluster to know which will decay vs persist. KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH has the longest tail.

text:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened by @Jules Meyer (oncall) — Elevated 429 rate + suspected client retry amplification. Tracking in incident channel. Please keep updates in thread.

Jules Meyer: Seeing a spike in 429s on hosted API in us-east. Traffic didn’t jump that much but request rate is climbing minute over minute, which makes me think client retries are fanning out. Anyone else looking at this?

Sean Gallagher: I’m here. Looking at gateway metrics now. 429 rate up ~6x in the last ~20m. Downstream saturation looks *secondary* (GPUs not pegged yet) but gateway QPS is higher than baseline.

Allison Grant: +1 to retry amplification hypothesis. The shape looks like bursts w/ sawtooth. Do we have breakdown by user-agent / SDK version?

Jules Meyer: UA strings show a ton of `redwood-ts/1.9.x` and some `redwood-py/0.14.x`. Hard to tell if it’s a new rollout or just one big ...

text:
Priya Shah: Quick follow-up from the GPU capacity postmortem. We need clear owners for the autoscale fixes and a tiny validation plan. Keep replies short.
Max Ortega: I can take scheduler tuning and the canary automation.
Ben Carter: I’ll own quota backstop + finance coordination.
Mei Lin: I’ll run replay validation and capture perf before/after.
Rafael Torres: I’ll do the 90-day incident pattern scan and propose two heuristics.
Priya Shah: Top items: 1) quota guardrail + cooldown, 2) scheduler thresholds & smoothing, 3) headroom test harness, 4) detection heuristics.
Max Ortega: Scheduler plan: reduce aggressive scale-down, add 30s smoothing, and set canary at 5% traffic. PR will include revert snippet.
Ben Carter: Quota plan: +10% burst buffer with hard cap and 20m cooldown. Need finance sign-off; drafting request now.
Mei Lin: Validation: staging replay 2h slow-ramp then 30m burst....

`if model_family in {"text","chat"} -> capability=chat` before route-based override. embeddings requests...

Christine Wong: overall: good improvement vs last drill on TTFU, but we still stumbled on (a) verifying impacted components before posting, and (b) anti-flap behavior causing a bunch of “degraded/operational/degraded” toggles in staging.

Allison Grant: +1. the anti-flap hysteresis isn’t being applied consistently across the new component signals. some panels are using raw 1m rates and others are smoothed. so depending on which dashboard folks check, they see different stories.

1) see paging alert (burn)

title: #incidents: 1747779033
preview:
Incident Bot: :warning: Near-miss declared (no customer-facing SEV) — elevated KMS timeouts + retry amplification observed in us-east. Tracking here for summary + follow-ups.

Sean Gallagher: Heads up: we had a near-miss ~10 mins ago where KMS started timing out and the platform amplified load via retries. We didn’t...
text:
Incident Bot: :warning: Near-miss declared (no customer-facing SEV) — elevated KMS timeouts + retry amplification observed in us-east. Tracking here for summary + follow-ups.

Sean Gallagher: Heads up: we had a near-miss ~10 mins ago where KMS started timing out and the platform amplified load via retries. We didn’t fully tip over, but we got uncomfortably close (5xx spiked + queue depth climbing). Posting details as we learn more.

Allison Grant: Do we have a dashboard snapshot? Want to capture before things normalize.

Sean Gallagher: Yep. Key panels:
- KMS p95 latency jumped from ~40ms -> 2-3s.
- `kms.requests` doubled within ~2 mins.
- `kms.errors{code=Timeout}` sustained at ~8-12% for ~6 mins.
- API 5xx went from baseline <0.2% -> peaked ~2.1% then recovered.
Likely retry storm: clients doing 3 retries *and* some upstream services have their own retry wrappers.

Rafael Mendes: Co...

Severity: SEV-2 (customer impact)
Customer: (redacted) Private VPC install
Signal: Prometheus targets down / dashboards blank
Owner: @Rafael Mendes

Rafael Mendes: seeing report from Support that all Redwood dashboards went empty ~30 min ago. Likely Prometheus isn’t scraping our pods.
Can someone confirm if this lines up with any recent change in the customer cluster? @Owen Phillips

Owen Phillips: yep. Customer did a “security hardening” pass and tightened NetworkPolicies this morning. After that, Grafana shows “No data” and Prometheus /targets is mostly DOWN for `redwood-*` jobs.

Sean Gallagher: got it. classic. if they went default-deny in namespaces, Prometheus can’t reach pod `:metrics` ports.
Do we know how Prometheus is deployed in their setup? In our min pack it’s ...

Mira Li: oncall SRE picking up. seeing sustained queue backlog in the serving frontend; p95 latency spiked to 2.1s. Routing shows traffic stuck on pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2.
Tom Anders: I can see autoscaler failed to add nodes; `autoscaler-controller` logs show `rate limit exceeded` talking to cloud provider quota API. Also some workers stuck in STATUS=RESTARTING.

```
> POST https://api.redwoodinference.com/v1/chat/completions

< Server: CorporateProxy
< X-Proxy-Error: disallowed_header

Blocked header: X-Redwood-Template
```
